description: To live in shared relationship, resources and responsibilities held in common. 'Shorai' names the deep sharing—not just proximity but intertwined lives.
sound_symbolism: The word shares outward. 'Sho' opens with collective spirit, sharing beginning. 'Ra' radiates with community spreading, bonds extending. Then 'i' rises to unified belonging.
grammatical_notes: 'lo mia shorai.' (We live communally.) The noun sense, communal living, comes with the verb by the event-noun rule. Related to 'punoa' (society) but more intimate; compare 'sila' (community).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Communal living—sharing resources, reducing waste, building solidarity.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Village life—the traditional pattern of interwoven households and shared labor.
